title: saponaria ocymoides var. alsinoides
intro: Saponaria ocymoides var. alsinoides, also known as rock soapwort or soapwort, is a perennial plant species in the carnation family that contains saponins, giving it its name. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its saponin content. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including soothing skin irritations and wound healing, due to its astringent and antiseptic properties. Saponaria ocymoides var. alsinoides can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused in hot water, or used as a topical application for its medicinal benefits.
